Step #1: Market Research
Remember that each step builds on the one before.
And this step, Market Research, is the foundation of everything.
Don't let the word "research" scare you. I've been helping people get over their innate fear of going
deep into the background of their market for decades.
It's actually easy and straightforward, once you have a simple guideline like this.
Most marketers skip this step, and it murders results all the way down the line. Early in my career, I
realized that spending even a little bit of time researching the "back story" of a market was like having
a secret weapon on your side.
You simply would know more about what was going on -- both in the market and in your prospect's
life--
-- than anyone else.
Everything else you do to generate sales flows from this step.
Again, it's not hard. It's just that most marketers have no clue what to look for, or where to find it.
After viewing this section's video lesson, you'll own advanced knowledge that will help you in
everything you do to make profits in your business.
It's critical that you complete the exercise, so this isn't just some "theory" floating around in your
head. Instead, by gently forcing your brain to consider what you know, and what you discover from a
brief "hot seat" on your market, you'll become adept at getting this step accomplished quickly.
That's the beauty of this system.
Copyright © JCSD Publishing LLC
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

Step #1: Market Research
The essential first step for
entrepreneurs and copywriters when
deciding how (or even if) you will
approach a specific market or niche
audience.
Copyright © JCSD Publishing LLC
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 2 of 7 --

Market Research
• Competition.
• Size of the game.
• Is there a market?
Copyright © JCSD Publishing LLC
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 3 of 7 --

Market Research
Competition: Identify them. Get on their
lists. Order from them, and pay attention to
the process you’re taken through. Sample
their goods and services. Call their offices,
speak to salesmen, ask questions. Google
them. Stalk them online.
Be a DETECTIVE. Dig for clues, answers
and insight.
Copyright © JCSD Publishing LLC
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 4 of 7 --

Market Research
Size of the game: How many
competitors are there, going after how
many prospects?
Copyright © JCSD Publishing LLC
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 5 of 7 --

Market Research
Is there a market? How many prospects are
there? Sometimes, markets are just too small
to break into efficiently. Other times, a “good
idea” just doesn’t have a large enough
responsive audience, or has been co-opted
already and given away free. (Use AdWords,
publication circulation and other resources to
gauge audience size.)
Never make an emotional decision based on
passion. Work from facts.
Copyright © JCSD Publishing LLC
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 6 of 7 --

Step #1:
Market Research
Step #1 Exercise:
Now I want you to conduct your own market research. You don't have to spend a lot
of time on this, just enough to be able to answer the following questions:
1. Is there a large enough market for your product or service?
2. Who are your major competitors?
3. What are some of the marketing methods they use?
Copyright © JCSD Publishing LLC
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com
